summary: First iteration E2E testing for GO Repair CRM. Ran 28 backend pytest tests (all passing after a single fix) + Playwright UI flows for admin/manager/technician. Found and fixed one MongoDB ObjectId serialization bug in /api/wallet/recharge, /api/wallet/credit, /api/wallet/debit. All critical flows now work including AI smart-assign via Claude Sonnet 4.5 returning real suggestions.

issues: Recharts BarChart logs 'width(-1) and height(-1)' console warning on initial mount - container sizing during fade-in. Chart still renders correctly after layout settles, but should set minHeight on ResponsiveContainer parent to avoid warning.
wallet-balance initializes to 0 on mount and updates after API fetch; no skeleton/loading state visible. Minor UX polish only.

action_items: (Optional polish) Fix recharts ResponsiveContainer width/height warning on Analytics by adding minHeight or explicit dimensions to chart wrapper div.
(Optional polish) Add loading skeleton to Wallet balance to avoid showing 0 flash before API returns.
critical_code_review_comments: BUG (fixed by testing agent): _wallet_txn in /app/backend/server.py returned txn dict directly after db.transactions.insert_one(txn), but motor mutates dict adding ObjectId _id. FastAPI json serialization then failed with 'ObjectId not iterable' on /wallet/recharge, /wallet/credit, /wallet/debit. Fixed by popping _id after insert_one before returning.
server.py is ~880 lines - acceptable for MVP but approaching split threshold (>700). Consider splitting leads/wallet/brand-kit/analytics into routers before adding more features.
CORS allow_origins=['*'] is fine for now with Bearer auth but switch to explicit origins in production.
No rate limiting on /auth/login (no brute-force protection). Mentioned in playbook instructions but not required per problem statement (no lockout requested).
Input on wallet_recharge accepts any positive integer - no upper bound (could DoS wallet balance). Consider a sane cap.
In assign_manager, wallet_txn + leads.update_one are not atomic - if lead update fails after debit, manager is charged without lead. MVP acceptable; document for phase-2.
updated_files: /app/backend/server.py (single line: txn.pop('_id', None) after insert_one in _wallet_txn)
test_credentials: All 6 seeded accounts in /app/memory/test_credentials.md verified working (admin/manager/technician).
seed_data_creation: Relied on existing seed (2 managers, 3 technicians, 5 leads). Test run created ~5 additional TEST_ prefixed leads; not cleaned up but tagged for easy identification.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend fix already applied. UI flows verified: login all 3 roles, role-based sidebar, leads list/filter/create, assign-manager (wallet debit), assign-technician, status updates, AI smart-assign returning live Claude suggestions with scores/reasons, wallet recharge (mocked), brand kit order flow, analytics, settings. Only cosmetic polish items remain.
rca of the issue: /api/wallet/recharge, /api/wallet/credit, /api/wallet/debit returned 500. RCA: motor's insert_one mutates the input dict to add MongoDB ObjectId as _id. The _wallet_txn helper returned that same dict directly, so FastAPI's jsonable_encoder saw ObjectId and crashed with 'ObjectId object is not iterable'. Mitigation: pop _id off the dict after insert_one before returning. One-line fix applied and all 28 backend tests now pass.
